action.hypothesis: After repeated failures from residual-only, routed, and bucketed residual variants at `K=24, EF=16`, the next severe-stagnation move should still be a fundamentally different existing architecture rather than another recipe tweak. `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` is the most informative next candidate because it tests whether a simpler single-stage soft-codebook composition can recover a better small-K tradeoff than the recently exhausted two-stage, routed, bucketed, and plain residual paths under the fixed modern signum recipe.
action.summary: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=24, EF=16` by switching to `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping optimizer, LR, aux loss, and preprocessing fixed at the current signum baseline. This continues severe-stagnation recovery with a materially different existing family, targets the valuable `K=24` frontier slot, and checks whether single-stage soft-codebook composition works better than the more structured variants that have recently failed.
action.self_review: This is a clean single-variable architecture probe and is more defensible than returning to optimizer or preprocessing after many misses. The main risk is that the broader soft-codebook family has already looked weaker than the bucketed winner, but the simpler one-stage variant has not been given a direct modern `K=24, EF=16` comparison and remains structurally different enough to justify one controlled test.
notes_to_memory: Round 164 continues severe-stagnation architecture search after bucketed low-rank residual also failed to improve the `K=24, EF=16` lane.

Hypothesis: `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` is the next materially different existing family because single-stage soft-codebook composition may scale down more gracefully at `K=24` than the recently exhausted two-stage, routed, bucketed, and plain residual variants under the fixed signum recipe.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` is competitive at `K=24`, follow with `K=16` on the same family to test whether the simpler codebook composition extends further down the frontier.
If it is close but still behind the bucketed soft-codebook backbone, test another fundamentally different existing family at `K=24` rather than returning to scalar recipe tweaks.
If it clearly loses at `K=24`, deprioritize plain single-stage soft-codebook residual coding in the current EF=16 regime and continue severe-stagnation search with the next distinct architecture.

result.description: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=24, EF=16` by switching to `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping optimizer, LR, aux loss, and preprocessing fixed at the current signum baseline. This continues severe-stagnation recovery with a materially different existing family, targets the valuable `K=24` frontier slot, and checks whether single-stage soft-codebook composition works better than the more structured variants that have recently failed.
result.self_review: This is a clean single-variable architecture probe and is more defensible than returning to optimizer or preprocessing after many misses. The main risk is that the broader soft-codebook family has already looked weaker than the bucketed winner, but the simpler one-stage variant has not been given a direct modern `K=24, EF=16` comparison and remains structurally different enough to justify one controlled test.
